title: Corporeal Beast/Strategies
wikitext:
{{External|rs}}
[[File:Fighting Corporeal Beast.png|thumb|400px|A group of players fighting the Corporeal Beast.]]
The [[Corporeal Beast]] is a high level [[boss]] monster in ''Old School RuneScape''.

==Requirements==
There are no strict requirements to killing the Corporeal Beast.

===Recommendations===
*High [[combat]] stats. Although not required, see the skill levels below for an indication of the advised stats:
**If using [[Melee]]:
***Level {{SCP|Hitpoints|85}} or higher
***Level {{SCP|Attack|94}} or higher
***Level {{SCP|Strength|94}} or higher
***Level {{SCP|Defence|94}} or higher
***Level {{SCP|Prayer|70}} or higher for the [[Piety]] prayer
**If using [[Ranged]]:
***Level {{SCP|Hitpoints|90}} or higher
***Level {{SCP|Ranged|90}} or higher
***Level {{SCP|Prayer|74}} or higher for the [[Rigour]] prayer

==Transportation==

The [[Corporeal Beast]] can be found in a cave at level 19-20 [[Wilderness]], which is surrounded by [[hostile]] [[ent]]s and [[black unicorn]]s. As the area is frequently visited by [[PKers]], entering through the [[Wilderness]] is not recommended. Instead, use a [[games necklace]] and teleport to the Corporeal Beast. The cave in which the Corporeal Beast resides is '''not''' part of the Wilderness.

==Mechanics==
===Attack styles===
The Corporeal Beast attacks with [[Melee]] and [[Magic]]. It attacks normally every 4 ticks, but it can occasionally attack 3 ticks after its last. It will use a melee attack 40% of the time provided you are in melee range, The magic attacks are all equally common with a 20% incidence in melee range or a 33% incidence outside of melee range.

Protect from Melee will block all damage from the melee attack and Protect from Magic will block 1/3 of the damage from the magic attacks. Despite this, it is recommended to always use Protect from Magic due to the magic attacks' higher damage, accuracy and frequency.

*The Melee attack is only used when the player is standing in Melee distance and can hit up to 33.
*The Magic attack has three variations. See the images below on how to identify the different variations.
**A regular Magic attack that can deal up to 65 damage.
**A Magic attack that can deal up to 55 damage and has a chance to drain Magic or Prayer by 1 or 2. The stat drain effect only takes place upon a successful hit. The boss is healed by half of the damage dealt, rounded down.
**A Magic attack that splits into six smaller projectiles that both have an AoE of 3x3. The main attack can hit up to 40 if it lands on top of you, or up to 30 if it lands next to you. Each of the projectiles can hit up to 30 if they land on top on you, or 20 if they land next to you. All of these can be dodged.

If the player steps under the Corporeal Beast, it may perform a stomp attack that will always deal 30–51 damage. This attack is on a timer that checks if any players are under the Corporeal Beast every 7 ticks (4.2 seconds). This attack cannot be negated with protection prayers.

<gallery captionalign="center" widths="350px" heights="300px" mode="packed">
Corporeal Beast - Magic attack (1).png|The Corporeal Beast's regular Magic attack.
Corporeal Beast - Magic attack (2).png|The Corporeal Beast's stat-draining Magic attack.
Corporeal Beast - Magic attack (3).png|The Corporeal Beast's AoE Magic attack.
</gallery>

===Dark energy core===
The [[Corporeal Beast]] can occasionally send out a dark energy core. It has a 1/8 chance of spawning whenever the Corporeal Beast is hit for at least 32 damage, or when it attacks while its hitpoints are under 1,000. {{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/884822755622957056|date=11 July 2017|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/103415|archivedate=26 May 2020|quote=1/8 chance, rolled: - when it attacks if its HP is under 1k - when it has received a large hit}}

The dark energy core leeches hitpoints from any players adjacent to it (in a 3x3 area) to heal the Corporeal Beast. It can deal 5–13 damage every 2 ticks (1.2 seconds). The dark energy core will jump to players if none are adjacent.

It can be weakened significantly by [[poisoning]] it. Once it has been poisoned, its attacks will be extremely slow. However, there must be at least one player around the dark energy core or else it will start hopping around again and will attack normally. If the dark energy core was [[Stun (status)|stunned]] but someone moved away from it and it starts hopping around again, it cannot be stunned again. It will keep dealing damage rapidly until it is killed. If the Corporeal Beast dies, the dark energy core will disappear.

The dark energy core will usually follow the player that stands northernmost. If no players are on the northern side, it will focus on the person that stands on the eastern side of the Corporeal Beast. It is usually advisable for the person who [[tank]]s or runs the core to stand north or east of the Corporeal Beast. Players that have experience with the Corporeal Beast typically know that there are specific game tiles that cause the core to prioritise and follow a player.

===Damage reduction===
The Corporeal Beast has a 50% damage reduction against any melee and ranged weapon that is not a [[Corpbane weapons|Corpbane weapon]]. These weapons must also be on the stab attack style. This damage reduction does not apply to magic, recoil damage (such as [[Vengeance]] or [[rings of recoil]]), [[Retribution]]. The damage reduction applies to the special effect of [[Ruby bolts (e)|enchanted ruby bolts]], before the cap of 100 damage is calculated.

There is also currently a bug that causes attacks from the [[dwarf multicannon]] to be reduced by 50% damage twice.

===Stat regeneration===
By default, the Corporeal Beast regenerates each drained stat once every 20 [[ticks]] (12 seconds), regardless of if there are players in the room. If there are no players in the cave, it will heal 25 hitpoints every 7 game ticks (4.2 seconds),{{fact|This was the value given on 30 June 2021, but a week later it was "slightly altered".}} and will fully heal after three minutes if no one has attacked it.

If there are eight or more players in the room simultaneously, it will regenerate an additional 5 Hitpoints per player every 7 ticks (4.2 seconds), which coincides with the stomp attack timer.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/782240276563783680|date=1 October 2016|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/102096|archivedate=26 May 2020|quote=5 [in all stats] by default, plus (5 x playercount) [Hitpoints] every 4.5 secs if there are 8+ players in the room.}}

===Ironmen room===
[[File:Corporeal Beast brazier.png|thumb|right|A lit brazier in the ironman room, indicating the world is occupied by another player]]
[[Ironmen]] with a [[combat level]] of 90 or higher have a separated Corporeal Beast room. All other accounts use the normal room. This is to reduce [[griefing]].

In addition, other players cannot assist in the encounter in any way: should someone damage or be attacked by the boss after the fight has been started by another player, kill credit will be invalidated for all players. This is to prevent players using other accounts to use stat-draining [[special attacks]] to speed up the kill. If someone has started the fight, a brazier in the lobby is lit with a blue flame.
{{clear}}

==Tips==
===Dark energy core===
[[File:Corporeal Beast dark core melee.gif|thumb|Using melee to permanently kill the dark core]]
To deal with the dark energy core, a number of strategies can be used:
*Use a [[crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]] that can fire [[emerald bolts (e)]]; if the [[special]] goes off, it will reduce the speed at which the dark energy core can sap players' hitpoints and prayer points. This requires a person to stay near it though, as failure to do so will result in the core bouncing around and the bolts' effects being removed.
*Move at least two tiles away whenever it jumps towards the player.
*Attack it as soon as it lands and immediately move away from it so it jumps; if it dies while midair, it will not respawn (this can be confirmed by its lack of ash drop). A [[dwarf multicannon]] can also be used to attack it midair, but this requires more timing as well as [[steel cannonball]]s.

===Stat draining===
While weak to [[stab]] attacks, the Corporeal Beast has a very high [[Defence]] level. A common method to lower its Defence is to use the [[Elder maul]], [[arclight]] and [[Bandos godsword]]'s [[special attack]] at the start of the fight.
*The [[Elder maul|Elder maul's]] special attack will lower the target's '''current''' Defence level by 35% on a hit.
*The [[Arclight|Arclight's]] special attack will lower the opponent's Strength, Attack, and Defence levels by 5%+1 on a [[successful hit]].
*The [[Bandos godsword|Bandos godsword's]] special attack will lower the opponent's combat levels equivalent to the damage hit in the following order: [[Defence]], [[Strength]], [[Prayer]], [[Attack]], [[Magic]], [[Ranged]].

The [[Elder maul|Elder maul's]] special attack is very useful in significantly increasing the player's damage output while fighting the Corporeal Beast.

The other special attacks are especially useful to players soloing the Corporeal Beast, as this will increase the player's damage output significantly while almost nullifying the Corporeal Beast's damage output. To achieve this, the special attacks have to be used in the following order:
#3 successful Elder maul special attacks. Although not required, this step is highly recommended to increase the accuracy of the arclight's special attacks.
#19 successful arclight special attacks. These attacks will completely drain the Corporeal Beast's Strength, Attack and Defence levels.
#A total damage of 200 with the Bandos godsword's special attack. These attacks will completely drain the Corporeal Beast's Magic level to almost completely negate its magical accuracy (although its maximum hit will be unaffected) and drastically reduce its overall damage output.
#*The special attack's stat reduction effect is calculated before the Corporeal Beast's 50% damage reduction, meaning 175 damage is enough to drain its 350 Magic levels. The remaining 25 damage is done to nullify the passive stat regeneration during the process.

It is recommended to bring item switches for gear that boost crush accuracy while using the Elder maul's special attacks, such as the [[tyrannical ring (i)]], [[mythical cape]] and [[dwarven helmet]].

For teams, it is often recommended to only reduce the Corporeal Beast's Defence level before proceeding to killing it, as it takes more time than it is worth to further reduce its stats in bigger groups.

===Player-owned house===
As most Corporeal Beast strategies heavily rely on using special attacks in quick succession, it is highly recommended to have a [[restoration pool]] or better in the [[player-owned house]]. Alternatively, players can pray at the [[Elidinis Statuette]] in [[Nardah]] after completion of {{SCP|Quest}} [[Spirits of the Elid]] quest. Both methods will restore the player's special attack energy.

To return to the Corporeal Beast quickly, it is highly recommended to have a [[basic jewellery box]] or better in the player-owned house. Alternatively, bring enough [[games necklace]]s in the inventory.

If no special attacks hit, players may typically restore their special attack through a [[restoration pool]] (or higher) and teleport back.

===Instance===
Players can pay {{Coins|200,000}} coins to host an [[instance]]. Players ranked as Captain or above in a [[clan chat]] can pay the instance cost for their clan. Once the instance is opened, anyone from the clan chat will be able to enter the instance for no cost. As long as there is one player present in the instance, it will remain open.

Note that ironmen cannot enter this instance.

==Solo and duo strategy==
===Stat draining===
For solo players (or small teams with low stats), killing the Corporeal Beast without massively reducing its stats can prove difficult as they may run out of food before killing it. Therefore, it may be more beneficial to drain its combat stats to zero by using their [[player-owned house]] to restore special attacks and teleport back repeatedly. An effective strategy is to:

* hit 3 [[Elder maul]] special attacks to lower its Defence to approximately a third, then
* hitting 19 [[Emberlight]] special attacks to reduce its melee stats, and then
* deal a total of 200 damage with [[Bandos godsword]] special attacks to reduce its Magic to 0.

Following this, the Corporeal Beast can be killed with relative ease and little food required. As a downside, the process is very slow; including the time to reduce its stats, solo players will take approximately 10 to 15 minutes per kill. However, it is the only realistic option for ultimate and hardcore [[Ironman Mode|ironmen]] to kill the boss.

===Quick-pool===

Reset (meaning teleport to a restore pool then tele back) until you land 1 Elder maul or Dragon warhammer spec. Then stop resetting, but continue attempting maul/hammer specs until you've landed 2-3 total or Corp reaches ~1700 HP, at which point use Voidwaker instead. Reset whenever your HP reaches Corp's max hit of 43. This method is significantly higher kills per hour than fully reducing Corp's stats, but is not recommended without high end gear & stats. [[Osmumten's Fang]], [[Voidwaker]] and an [[Elder Maul]] or [[Dragon Warhammer]] are particularly essential. 
* Swap to maximum crush attack bonus gear when using Elder maul or DWH specs
* Swap to strength boosting gear when using Voidwaker specs ([[Amulet of rancour]] and [[Ultor ring]] or [[Berserker ring (i)]])
* Food is not needed, and teleporting to a bank during kills to restock on food will slow down kills compared to only teleporting to a restore pool
* Use [[Spellbook Swap]] to maintain [[Vengeance]] and [[Thralls]] constantly
* If you spellbook swap after red clicking Corp upon entering the arena, you can use the spell while running to avoid tick delay
* Mage defence (and to a lesser extent Crush defence) is beneficial

===Death piling===
An alternative strategy is only lowering its Defence and dying in the boss room with an inventory full of food; because of the changes to [[death]] mechanics, it will stay on the floor for 60 minutes. This will supply the player with enough food to outlast the boss. This strategy requires more effort and supplies, but it results in faster kills. Gear with a higher emphasis on magic defence is highly recommended, as the Corporeal Beast will be able to deal damage as normal. 

Though the beast will regenerate its stats throughout the fight, it usually does not have enough time to regenerate much before it is killed. However, as it regenerates its Magic level, the chance of its attacks becomes more likely, and its maximum hit remains the same regardless of its Magic level. Therefore, using [[Protect from Magic]] and gear with high magic defence is beneficial even if its stats are reduced to prevent taking high damage hits.

===Quick-banking===
Another option for players with access to [[Osmumten's fang|Osmumten's Fang]] is to bring a full inventory of [[Cooked karambwan|karambwan]], attack the boss without draining defence (or with only one or two Elder maul hits), teleport out using a fast bank teleport like the [[Crafting cape|Crafting Cape]] to refill on food, then teleport back to the Corporeal Beast's lair as quickly as possible. The boss will heal for 100-150 hp in the time you are outside the room, but this is easily overwhelmed by the damage you can do to the boss before running out of food, with most kills requiring 3-4 food refills. Because of the Fang's high accuracy even against a Corporeal Beast with full stats, this strategy gets rapid kills without the effort of de-gearing, dying in the boss room, and re-gearing every kill. You will take large amounts of damage using this method, burning through 60+ karambwan per kill, and you should plan to eat any time your HP drops below 70, but the low tick delay on eating a karambwan without eating another food at the same time means eating does not significantly decrease your DPS. The [[Ring of suffering (ri)|ring of suffering (ri)]] and [[Amulet of blood fury|Amulet of Blood Fury]] are better options than the traditional DPS rings and amulets, to decrease the amount of time spent eating and recoil the boss' attacks. This strategy will not work with weapons other than the Fang.

===Solo with an alt===

For players with access to an [[Main account|alt]] that has 82 attack and reasonably high strength, defence, and magic levels (85+), doloing the Corporeal Beast (fighting it on two accounts at the same time) with [[Osmumten's fang]] offers far more kills per hour than other solo methods (~21 with a maxed alt), because it eliminates the need to both drain the boss and leave the room for food. It is recommended to bring an [[elder maul]] or [[dragon warhammer]] on each account and reset with house pools until at least one spec lands. From there, each account's inventory of food should be enough to simply kill Corp head-on if both are using [[Osmumten's fang]]. An inventory of mostly [[Cooked karambwan|karambwans]] with only about 4-6 [[Food/Fast foods|normal food]] for [[Food/Fast foods|combo eating]] is ideal to minimize the high amounts of attack delay that will be incurred. If Corp is targeting one account heavily, one may drop some food on the non-targeted account as a precaution, then pick it up on the targeted account 1 minute later. 

It is recommended the player familiarizes themselves with permanently eliminating the dark core using melee before attempting the dolo method, as it is a consistent and quick way to eliminate the core and leaving it alive will greatly complicate the kill when playing on two accounts. The easiest way to permanently kill the dark core in a dolo is to keep your two accounts spaced out as they attack Corp until the dark core spawns. When you see the dark core spawn, stop attacking Corp by equipping each account's [[elder maul]]/[[dragon warhammer]]. Watch the dark core to see which account it jumps to, and hover your cursor under that account's feet until the option to attack the core appears. Attack it right away and then immediately run away from the core. If the hit is fatal, the core should jump away after you kill it and permanently disappear when it lands. If this fails to kill the core permanently, simply try again. Once the core has been killed, continue to fang Corp on both accounts, eating as necessary, until it dies.

A 3rd account may also be used for backup [[elder maul]]/[[dragon warhammer]]/[[Tonalztics of Ralos]] specs, as well as casting [[vengeance other]], [[heal other]], and [[energy transfer]] on the dps accounts (for [[voidwaker]] specs). This can result in up to 26 kills per hour. 

It is recommended to bring either a crafting cape or [[amulet of eternal glory]] on the accounts to facilitate faster banking. Travelling through the house to bank is about 10-11 seconds slower per kill than using one of these alternatives.

===Melee - Solo and Duo===

This section assumes that the player is using the stat draining method to completely reduce the Corporeal Beast's damage output. The setup shown in this section currently costs approximately {{Coins|{{GEP|Torva full helm}} + {{GEP|Amulet of rancour}} + {{GEP|Zamorakian spear}} + {{GEP|Masori body (f)}} + {{GEP|Masori body (f)}} + {{GEP|Hydra leather}} + {{GEP|Primordial boots}} + {{GEP|Avernic treads}} + {{GEP|Berserker ring}} + {{GEP|Elder maul}} + {{GEP|Avernic defender hilt}} + {{GEP|Inquisitor's great helm}} + {{GEP|Tyrannical ring}} + {{GEP|Tormented synapse}} + {{GEP|Bandos godsword}}}} coins (or {{Coins|{{GEP|Helm of neitiznot}} + {{GEP|Basilisk jaw}} + {{GEP|Amulet of rancour}} + {{GEP|Zamorakian spear}} + {{GEP|Karil's leathertop}} + {{GEP|Karil's leatherskirt}} + {{GEP|Hydra leather}} + {{GEP|Primordial boots}} + {{GEP|Berserker ring}} + {{GEP|Dragon warhammer}} + {{GEP|Zombie helmet}} + {{GEP|Tyrannical ring}} + {{GEP|Bandos godsword}}}} without the [[Inquisitor's great helm]], [[Emberlight]], [[avernic defender]], [[Torva full helm]], [[Masori body (f)]], [[Masori chaps (f)]], [[Avernic treads]], and [[Elder maul]], which are only marginally better for their high cost). See the table below for an overview of equipment alternatives in case this setup is not accessible or too expensive.

===Notes===
*[[Noxious halberd]] surpasses [[Osmumten's fang]] if Corp's defence level is below 70. As a result, it is recommended for methods that fully or near-fully reduce Corp's defence. 
*Bring an [[elder maul]]/[[dragon warhammer]] for its special attack. Optionally, bring a [[mythical cape]], [[tyrannical ring (i)]] or [[brimstone ring]], and [[inquisitor's great helm]] or [[Zombie helmet]] as item switches to increase the elder maul/dragon warhammer's accuracy, as well as the best available defender if using a dragon warhammer.
*Bring the [[Emberlight]] for its special attack (the [[Arclight]] will provide the same special attack at lower accuracy for much lower cost.) Optionally, bring the best available defender, as well as a [[bellator ring]], [[warrior ring (i)]] or [[brimstone ring]] and [[warrior helm]] to increase the Emberlight or arclight's accuracy for the first ten special attacks (after which gear that increases strength bonus becomes more effective).
*Bring a [[Bandos godsword]] for its special attack.
*Bring a [[divine super combat potion]] or [[super combat potion]] for boosting combat stats.
*Bring prayer restore potions to restore prayer points, unless [[prayer flicking]].
*Bring high healing food as backup; typically only a couple are needed per kill.
*Bring a teleport to house (either runes for casting [[Teleport to House]], [[Teleport to house (tablet)|house tablets]] or [[Construct. cape|Construction cape]]).
*Bring [[rune pouch]] with [[runes]] for [[Vengeance]] if using house tablets or Construction cape.

===Notes, gear, and inventory===
*See the above [[Corporeal_Beast/Strategies#Quick-pool|quick-pool]] section for a more detailed outline of the strategy.
*Due to the accuracy and damage of Corp's magic attacks, magic defence is highly valued, sometimes at the expense of strength bonus. In addition to costing time, using the pool/statue will heal the boss, so minimising downtime is essential.
*Bring an [[elder maul]] or [[dragon warhammer]] for their special attack, as well as your maximum crush bonus gear such as [[Inquisitor's armour]], [[mythical cape]] and [[tyrannical ring (i)]]
*Bring a [[Voidwaker]] for its special attack, and an [[ultor ring]] or [[berserker ring (i)]] swap when using VW specs
*On the [[Lunar spellbook]], bring runes for [[Spellbook Swap]], [[Vengeance]] and [[Thralls]]
*Bring a [[divine super combat potion]] or [[super combat potion]] to boost combat stats
*[[Saturated heart]] or [[Imbued heart]] are used to boost magic defence
*Bring your fastest teleport to a restore pool to restore HP and Spec ([[Desert amulet 4]] > [[Nardah teleport]] > [[Teleport to house (tablet)]])
*Bring games necklaces to teleport to Corp, and a fast teleport to a bank for in between kills
*With 70+ Prayer level, prayer potions should never be necessary since you're forced to use restore pools frequently

See the above [[Corporeal_Beast/Strategies#Solo_with_an_alt|dolo]] section for more information. The gear setup is identical for both accounts and focuses for DPS over defence in many slots because the added defence is unnecessary. The food both accounts are carrying is more than enough to see the kill through in this equipment following the outlined strategy, so shedding DPS in these slots only slows kills down.

This method focuses on draining only a part of Corporeal Beast's defence and dying in the room to have an extra supply of food. As the majority of the time is used on killing the boss as opposed to draining its stats, this method is slightly faster but requires more high-end gear and is more resource and skill intensive.

Before equipping this setup, use three [[Elder maul]] and four to six [[Emberlight]] special attacks on the boss, then purposefully die within the room (preferably close to the entrance) with a full inventory of [[cooked karambwan]] two or three times; unless picked up and dropped again, these will stay on the floor for a full hour before despawning and serve as extra supplies that will be needed due to the boss's high damage output.

The setup is based on a mix of magic defence and strength gear. For [[Ironman Mode|ironmen]], including the time to obtain the extra food used, this method may not be faster effective kills if you do not have some of the demonstrated equipment such as [[Armadyl armour]]. It also requires more practice to become a consistent method; a large portion of mitigating damage taken, and therefore needing to heal less which takes time away from damaging the boss, is switching between a shield and a magic staff (for their added magic defence) and the spear between attacks, and optionally switching between a magic prayer (e.g. [[Augury]]) and [[Piety]]. Making mistakes is also more punishing due to the increased risk of dying and effectively resetting the fight.

==Notes==
*An [[imbued heart]] or a [[magic potion]] is recommended to increase Magic level which subsequently increases magic defence and reduces damage taken.
*[[Vengeance]] and [[ring of suffering (i)]] charged with [[rings of recoil]] are highly recommended.
*A shield and a staff or wand with high magic defence are highly recommended for "shield switching" (as detailed above); examples include [[Elysian spirit shield|elysian]]/[[spectral spirit shield]], [[twisted buckler]], and [[dragonfire ward]], and [[Master wand|master]]/[[kodai wand]], [[staff of the dead]] and [[trident of the swamp]].
*Cooked karambwans are more effective than higher healing food due to their lower delay after eating: as opposed to most other foods, a karambwan adds a 2-tick attack delay after eating as opposed to 3 ticks. They are also more easily obtained in bulk.
*A [[dwarf multicannon]] is recommended to deal more damage and deal with the dark energy core while focusing all player damage on the boss; the steel cannonball drops from the boss should largely sustain them.
*It is recommended to have the supply piles six tiles east of the entrance and lure the boss to the entrance before starting the fight, then moving east of it. The Corporeal Beast moves randomly during the fight, which will drag the player away from the supply piles if it is not trapped between the player and the entrance.

This is the best-in-slot available equipment for killing the Corporeal Beast in small teams. The setup shown in this section currently costs approximately {{Coins|{{GEP|Torva full helm}} + {{GEP|Amulet of rancour}} + {{GEP|Osmumten's fang}} + {{GEP|Masori body (f)}} + {{GEP|Masori chaps (f)}} + {{GEP|Hydra leather}} + {{GEP|Primordial boots}} + {{GEP|Avernic treads}} + {{GEP|Berserker ring}} + {{GEP|Dragon warhammer}} + {{GEP|Avernic defender hilt}} + {{GEP|Tyrannical ring}} + {{GEP|Inquisitor's great helm}}}} coins (or {{Coins|{{GEP|Helm of neitiznot}} + {{GEP|Basilisk jaw}} + {{GEP|Amulet of rancour}} + {{GEP|Osmumten's fang}} + {{GEP|Karil's leathertop}} + {{GEP|Karil's leatherskirt}} + {{GEP|Hydra leather}} + {{GEP|Primordial boots}} + {{GEP|Berserker ring}} + {{GEP|Dragon warhammer}} + {{GEP|Tyrannical ring}} + {{GEP|Dwarven helmet}}}} without the [[Inquisitor's great helm]], [[Avernic defender]], [[Torva full helm]], [[Masori body (f)]], [[Masori chaps (f)]], and [[Avernic treads]] which are only marginally better for their high cost). See the table below for an overview of equipment alternatives in case this setup is not accessible or too expensive.

===Notes===
*Bring an [[Elder maul]] for its special attack. Bring a [[mythical cape]], [[tyrannical ring]] and [[inquisitor's great helm]] or [[Zombie helmet]] as item switches to increase the Elder maul's accuracy.
*Bring a [[divine super combat potion]] or [[super combat potion]] for boosting combat stats.
*Bring prayer restore potions to restore prayer points.
*Bring high healing food as well as [[cooked karambwan]]s for [[combo food]].
*[[Rune pouch]] with [[runes]] for the [[Teleport to House]] spell.
*Bring a teleport to house (either runes for casting [[Teleport to House]], [[Teleport to house (tablet)|house tablets]] or [[Construct. cape|Construction cape]]).
*Bring a [[rune pouch]] with [[runes]] for [[Vengeance]] if using house tablets or Construction cape.

The setup shown in this section currently costs approximately {{Coins|{{GEP|Torva full helm}} + {{GEP|Amulet of rancour}} + {{GEP|Osmumten's fang}} + {{GEP|Torva platebody}} + {{GEP|Torva platelegs}} + {{GEP|Hydra leather}} + {{GEP|Primordial boots}} + {{GEP|Avernic treads}} + {{GEP|Berserker ring}} + {{GEP|Avernic defender hilt}}}} coins. ===Notes===
*Some clans will not allow strength boosting armour or [[infernal cape]]s during mass events. If that is the case, [[Masori armour]] and [[fire cape]] are the best options.
*Bring a [[divine super combat potion]] for boosting combat stats.
*Bring prayer restore potions to restore prayer points.
*Bring high healing food as well as [[cooked karambwan]]s for [[combo food]].
*Bring a teleport to house (either runes for casting [[Teleport to House]], [[Teleport to house (tablet)|house tablets]] or [[Construct. cape|Construction cape]]).
